methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected pollen data, including normality tests, nonparametric tests, multivariate regression tree analysis, and linear regression analysis.

steps.description: Normality of the dataset was assessed with the Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Chi-square tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform normality tests using Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Chi-square

steps.description: Since the variables did not follow normal distribution (p < 0.05), nonparametric tests and methods that do not require normality were applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply nonparametric tests due to non-normal distribution

steps.description: The Kruskal-Wallis test, used to determine whether there were significant differences in the total amount of pollen recorded annually at each station, was performed using SPSS 17.0.
outputs: significance of differences in pollen amounts

steps.description: Similarity of sites in terms of pollen composition and concentrations was assessed with the help of multivariate regression tree (MRT) method.
steps.searchable_summary: use MRT method to assess site similarity in pollen composition

steps.description: The overall fit of the tree is specified as relative error (RE; SSD in the clusters divided by SSD of the undivided data), and the predictive accuracy is assessed by CV RE (crossvalidated relative error).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate RE and CV RE for MRT model

steps.description: The finally selected tree was the most complex model within one standard error (1 SE) from the best predictive tree, using 2000 multiple cross-validations, to stabilize CV RE.
steps.searchable_summary: select final MRT model using 2000 cross-validations

steps.description: Simple linear regression analysis was used to describe trends in the API of selected pollen seasons.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression analysis on pollen season API

methods.description: Monitoring of airborne pollen using volumetric spore traps at various stations across Portugal and its islands.

steps.description: Airborne pollen monitoring during the period 2003-2012 was performed using volumetric spore traps of the Hirst (1952) design at all seven stations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect airborne pollen data using Hirst spore traps
